abstract: Many scientific disciplines have achieved major breakthroughs by adopting online platforms that organize, structure, and analyze data at a level of detail that traditional journals simply cannot accommodate. In this paper, we introduce OpenML: an open-science platform designed for the machine learning community to share and organize data, code, and experiments in fine detail. By moving beyond summarized PDF reports to a structured, networked commons, OpenML allows researchers to work more efficiently, increase their professional visibility, and collaborate globally to tackle complex challenges. We explore how OpenML fits into the broader landscape of networked science and outline the specific advantages it offers to researchers, students, and practitioners alike.
